name: Brownie Cake in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: vergie@juno.com (Vergie A Ewing)
This recipe makes 2 jars.
1. Sterilize two 1-pint, straight-sided, wide-mouth canning jars, lids, and rings by boiling for 10 minutes; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if desired). Set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine butter or margarine, water, and cocoa powder; heat and stir until the butter or margarine is melted and the mixture is well-blended. Remove from heat and stir in the dry ingredients. Add buttermilk, beaten egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth. Stir in nuts.
4. Pour the mixture into the prepared canning jars; place the jars onto a cookie sheet.
5. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes or until a pick inserted deep into each cake comes out clean. Remove cakes from the oven, one at a time.
7. Place a lid on each jar, then a ring; screw the ring down tightly. Place jars on a counter to cool. You'll know the jars have sealed when you hear the "plinking" sound. Store in a cool, dry place.
